title: hymenochlaena decurrens
intro: Hymenochlaena decurrens, also known as the Native Pepper Bush, is a small shrub native to Australia and Tasmania. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hymenochlaena decurrens include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate colds, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. Hymenochlaena decurrens can be prepared as a tea or infusion, with leaves and stems being used in herbal remedies.
